summary: Iteration 2: Validated NSE whitelist enforcement + vuln preset + PDF export on NetScan. Updated existing pytest file to expect 7 scan types (including 'vuln') and added 9 new tests covering NSE whitelist (block/allow/--script= form/--script-args/-iL), vuln preset creation, and PDF export (success + 404). Ran full suite: 20/21 passed. One failure is a timing flake (vuln NSE scan didn't complete within 180s due to parallel test load); increased timeout to 300s for future runs. All functional assertions on vuln preset (flags, label) pass. Frontend verified end-to-end on mobile viewport: 7 scan cards rendered incl. scan-type-vuln, tapping vuln selects it, custom card exposes custom-flags-input + whitelist helper text, quick scan on 127.0.0.1 navigates to detail, status reaches COMPLETED, export-pdf-btn and delete-scan-btn both appear in header, tapping export opens PDF in a new tab.

minor.issue: NSE vuln scan on 127.0.0.1 sometimes takes >180s in this container (one run took 94s standalone, 180s+ under parallel test load). Functionality is fine; test deadline bumped to 300s.

action_items: Optional: consider reducing vuln preset NSE set or --top-ports to speed up scans in CI (currently --script vuln,vulners,http-title --top-ports 200).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~628 lines and starting to mix concerns (models, parsing, heuristics, PDF rendering, routes). Split render_scan_pdf and analyze_findings into their own modules before it grows past 700 lines.
render_scan_pdf imports reportlab inside the function (good for cold start) but does so every request; move imports to module top once >0 requests in practice is expected.
Whitelist is hardcoded constant; consider making it configurable via env for future flexibility.
seed_data_creation: None (tests create and clean up their own scans).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file covers all current features. NSE whitelist enforcement and --script= form both validated. PDF export returns %PDF-1.x with Content-Disposition netscan-<target>-<id8>.pdf. Frontend uses testIDs: hero-box, target-input, scan-type-{key} (7 keys), custom-flags-input, start-scan-btn, scan-status, export-pdf-btn, delete-scan-btn. For web PDF export the frontend uses window.open so Playwright should use context.expect_page to catch the popup; do NOT wait_for_load_state('domcontentloaded') on the PDF tab — binary responses don't fire it.
rca_of_issue: Single test failure test_vuln_scan_eventually_completes was a timeout (180s) flake — vuln NSE scan ran concurrently with other tests and exceeded deadline. Not a functional bug; creation + flags + label all validated. Deadline extended to 300s in the file.
